title: itertools: concurrent repr() of a shared count() races count_repr's plain read of lz->cnt against count_next's atomic advance
description: In the free-threaded build count_next advances a fast-mode count()'s counter lz->cnt with an atomic compare-exchange (itertoolsmodule.c:3599), but count_repr reads the same field with a plain non-atomic load (itertoolsmodule.c:3612). Sharing a count() across threads while some repr() it and others next() it is a data race (atomic write vs plain read) on an operation callers treat as read-only.
sites: Modules/itertoolsmodule.c count_repr:3612 (plain read of lz->cnt in fast-mode repr branch)
race_kind: atomic/non-atomic mismatch (writer atomic, reader plain) on a shared counter field
shared_state: countobject.cnt (Py_ssize_t fast-mode counter; written atomically by count_next, read plainly by count_repr)
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; aligned Py_ssize_t, no torn read; worst case is a stale repr value; technically UB data race on read-only-looking repr())
suggested_fix: read lz->cnt in count_repr via _Py_atomic_load_ssize_relaxed(&lz->cnt) (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_SSIZE_RELAXED), matching the relaxed atomic load count_next already uses at itertoolsmodule.c:3591
notes: Real bug: incomplete free-threading atomic conversion of the count object. count_next was hardened (relaxed-atomic load + CAS on lz->cnt, plus a critical section for the slow-mode fast->slow transition), but count_repr's fast-mode read of lz->cnt was left as a plain load. TSan's SUMMARY top-frame alternates between count_repr:3612 and count_next:3599/_Py_atomic_compare_exchange_ssize depending on scheduling; same race. Fleet vehicle drove the write side via list(count()) (list_extend_iter -> count_next); the minimal repro uses next() instead to avoid the infinite list(). Audit other itertools objects for the same atomic-write/plain-read asymmetry. FILED from umbrella #153852 -> python/cpython#153908 (FIXED (merged PR python/cpython#153917; 3.14/3.15 backports #153952/#153951 open)) [2026-07-18]. FLEET-08 (2026-07-18): fusil's op-h shares a BIG-INT count(10**18, 2), which drives the SLOW mode (lz->long_cnt, a PyObject*, not the fast lz->cnt ssize). count_repr plain-BORROWS lz->long_cnt and reprs it (PyUnicode_FromFormat '%R') while count_nextlong writes lz->long_cnt = stepped_up and returns the OLD long_cnt (ref transferred to the next() caller, which then DECREFs it). So besides the plain pointer race `count_nextlong | count_repr` (142 veh), count_repr's PyObject_Repr on the borrowed old long_cnt USE-AFTER-FREES it when the caller frees it -> 3 UAF faces (`_Py_TYPE_impl | _PyMem_DebugRawAlloc` 7 veh, `_Py_TYPE_impl | _PyMem_DebugRawFree` 1, `long_to_decimal_string_internal | _PyMem_DebugRawFree` 1) -- all have count_repr on the stack (frame ~#6). CAVEAT: those 3 faces are GENERIC-shaped (Py_TYPE / long repr vs obmalloc DebugRaw); a future NON-count repr-UAF would mislabel as TSAN-0006, so verify count_repr is on the stack. The fleet build predates #153917 (no _Py_atomic in count) so this is the pre-fix state; OPEN ITEM: re-check the slow-mode long_cnt repr-vs-nextlong path on a POST-#153917 build -- if the fix's critical section covered only the fast->slow transition and left count_repr/count_nextlong's long_cnt access unguarded, the slow-mode race + UAF is an incomplete-fix residual worth a note on #153908.

related_issues.note: Modules/itertoolsmodule.c is UNCHECKED on the gh-116738 audit list; count_repr reads lz->cnt plainly (count_next uses a CAS) on current main.

assessment: real, new — count_next CAS hardened but count_repr reader missed; count not covered by the above
fleet02_notes: fleet-02 variant: the count_next CAS write-side vs count_repr read (same race, parser picked the atomic frame)
